subject: SEC() annotation suffixes
old: SEC("xdp1") or SEC("xdp_prog1") style random suffixes
truth: Stop using random suffixes in SEC() annotations; use only canonical annotation names like SEC("xdp") without suffixes.
quote: Random suffixes no longer supported: Programs must use canonical annotations only (e.g., SEC("xdp"), not SEC("xdp1") or SEC("xdp_prog1"))

from_fact: sec("xdp1") or sec("xdp_prog1") style random suffixes

subject: SEC("maps") legacy map definitions
truth: Stop using SEC("maps") for map definitions; migrate to SEC(".maps") with BTF definitions.
quote: Legacy map definitions removed: SEC("maps") support dropped; users must migrate to SEC(".maps") with BTF definitions

subject: Program identification method
old: section names to uniquely identify BPF programs
new: C function names to uniquely identify BPF programs
truth: Use C function names instead of section names to uniquely identify BPF programs.
quote: Program identification: Use C function names instead of section names to uniquely identify BPF programs

from_fact: section names to uniquely identify bpf programs
c function names to uniquely identify bpf programs

subject: BPF program pinning behavior
new: BPF program names used for pinning
truth: BPF program names are now used for pinning instead of section names.
quote: Pinning behavior change: BPF program names now used for pinning instead of section names

from_fact: bpf program names used for pinning

subject: Integer-returning API error reporting
new: return negative -Exxx directly and set positive errno to Exxx
truth: Integer-returning APIs now return negative -Exxx directly as result and set positive errno to Exxx.
quote: Integer-returning APIs: Return negative -Exxx directly as result and set positive errno to Exxx

from_fact: return negative -exxx directly and set positive errno to exxx

subject: Destructors NULL acceptance
old: NULL pointers caused crashes in destructors like bpf_object__close() and btf__free()
truth: Destructors like bpf_object__close() and btf__free() now safely accept NULL pointers without requiring guards.
quote: Destructors like bpf_object__close() and btf__free() now safely accept NULL pointers without requiring guards.

from_fact: null pointers caused crashes in destructors like bpf_object__close() and btf__free()
associative: safe to pass NULL to close

subject: AF_XDP APIs (xsk.h)
truth: Stop using xsk.h APIs from libbpf; they have been moved to the separate libxdp project.
quote: AF_XDP consolidation: xsk.h APIs moved to separate libxdp project

subject: Getter function naming
truth: Stop using get_ prefix in getter functions; use bpf_program__type() instead of bpf_program__get_type().
quote: Naming standardization: Getters omit 'get_' prefix (bpf_program__type()), setters include 'set_' (bpf_program__set_type())

subject: Multiple map creation functions
truth: Stop using the six legacy map creation functions; use bpf_map_create() instead.
quote: bpf_map_create() supersedes six similar map creation functions

subject: Multiple program loading APIs
truth: Stop using the multiple legacy program loading APIs; use bpf_prog_load() instead.
quote: bpf_prog_load() consolidates multiple program loading APIs

subject: bpf_probe_read_{kernel,user}()
truth: Stop using bpf_probe_read_kernel() and bpf_probe_read_user(); they are downgraded to bpf_probe_read() on older kernels.
quote: bpf_probe_read_{kernel, user}() downgraded to bpf_probe_read() on older kernels

subject: libbpf strict mode default
truth: Starting in libbpf v1.0, LIBBPF_STRICT_ALL behavior is the default; you must explicitly opt out if you rely on pre-1.0 loose behavior.
quote: This will be the default behavior in libbpf v1.0.

subject: LIBBPF_STRICT_NONE mode
truth: LIBBPF_STRICT_NONE mode will not be supported in libbpf v1.0; you must update code to handle LIBBPF_STRICT_ALL mode.
quote: It won't be supported anymore in v1.0, please update your code so that it handles LIBBPF_STRICT_ALL mode before libbpf v1.0.

subject: pointer-returning APIs error behavior
old: pointer-returning APIs (e.g. bpf_object__open_file, btf__parse, perf_buffer__new) return special non-NULL error pointers (ERR_PTR encoding error code)
new: pointer-returning APIs return NULL on error, with errno set to the error code
truth: With LIBBPF_STRICT_CLEAN_PTRS, pointer-returning APIs return NULL instead of ERR_PTR error pointers; check for NULL and use -errno to get the error code.
quote: When LIBBPF_STRICT_CLEAN_PTRS mode is enabled, libbpf will no longer return such special pointer. Instead NULL is returned, as one would expect.

from_fact: pointer-returning apis (e.g. bpf_object__open_file, btf__parse, perf_buffer__new) return special non-null error pointers (err_ptr encoding error code)
pointer-returning apis return null on error, with errno set to the error code

subject: libbpf_get_error() function
old: libbpf_get_error() is the recommended way to check pointer-returning APIs for errors
truth: libbpf_get_error() is going to be eventually deprecated and removed in libbpf 1.0; switch to NULL checks and errno.
quote: libbpf_get_error() is going to be eventually deprecated and removed in libbpf 1.0 release, so eventually you'd need to turn on LIBBPF_STRICT_CLEAN_PTRS and switch code to NULL checks and errno (if necessary).

from_fact: libbpf_get_error() is the recommended way to check pointer-returning apis for errors

subject: error return convention for int-returning APIs
old: return -1 on error with errno set
truth: int-returning libbpf APIs now return -errno directly instead of -1; applications must check for < 0 instead of == -1.
quote: for int-returning APIs that can fail, actual error is returned directly as -Exxx and errno is set to Exxx;

from_fact: return -1 on error with errno set

subject: libbpf error handling with LIBBPF_STRICT_DIRECT_ERRS
old: libbpf returned -1 on error and set errno
new: libbpf always returns the actual negative error code directly and also sets errno
truth: With LIBBPF_STRICT_DIRECT_ERRS enabled, libbpf always returns the actual negative error code directly instead of -1, so checks like 'err == -1' will break and must be changed to 'err < 0'.
quote: With `LIBBPF_STRICT_DIRECT_ERRS` enabled, libbpf always sets `errno` *and* always return the actual error code directly. So if your code relies on -1 checks like below, it will become broken

from_fact: libbpf returned -1 on error and set errno
libbpf always returns the actual negative error code directly and also sets errno

subject: High-level libbpf APIs now set errno
old: high-level APIs returned error codes directly but did not set errno
new: high-level APIs now also set errno with a positive error code
truth: High-level libbpf APIs (from libbpf.h and btf.h) now also set errno with a positive error code when returning a negative error code, making errno-based error handling possible but discouraged.
quote: High-level APIs (mostly those that are coming from `libbpf.h` and `btf.h` headers) have always returned error codes directly, so no changes there. But now they, for uniformity, also set `errno` with a positive error code

from_fact: high-level apis returned error codes directly but did not set errno
high-level apis now also set errno with a positive error code

subject: SEC annotation xdp_cpumap
truth: The SEC annotation 'xdp_cpumap' is deprecated and must be replaced with 'xdp/cpumap'.
quote: | `SEC("xdp_cpumap")`   | `SEC("xdp/cpumap")`  |

subject: SEC annotation xdp_devmap
truth: The SEC annotation 'xdp_devmap' is deprecated and must be replaced with 'xdp/devmap'.
quote: | `SEC("xdp_devmap")`   | `SEC("xdp/devmap")`  |

subject: SEC annotation classifier
truth: The SEC annotation 'classifier' is deprecated and must be replaced with 'tc'.
quote: | `SEC("classifier")`   | `SEC("tc")`          |

subject: low-level BPF APIs (bpf_*)
old: return -1 with errno set, or return -Exxx without setting errno
new: return -errno directly AND still set errno
truth: Low-level bpf_* APIs now return the negative errno value directly as the result, instead of returning -1 with errno set or returning -Exxx without errno.
quote: We’ll standardize on low-level APIs returning the value of -errno directly as a result.

from_fact: return -1 with errno set, or return -exxx without setting errno
return -errno directly and still set errno

subject: BPF program section name parsing
old: non-conforming section names logged a warning
new: non-conforming section names cause hard errors
truth: In v1.0, non-conforming BPF program section names that previously logged a warning will now cause hard errors.
quote: In v1.0, instead of logging a warning, such 'violations' will cause hard errors.

from_fact: non-conforming section names logged a warning
non-conforming section names cause hard errors

subject: error return convention for pointer-returning (constructor) APIs
old: return ERR_PTR-encoded error on failure (non-NULL pointer with encoded error)
new: return NULL on failure, errno set to Exxx
truth: Constructor APIs (bpf_object__open, btf__parse, bpf_program__attach, etc.) now return NULL on error instead of an ERR_PTR-encoded error pointer.
quote: All constructor-like APIs that return a new "object", e.g., `bpf_object__open()` variants, `btf__parse()`, `bpf_program__attach()` returning bpf_link, etc., will start returning NULL, not an error code encoded as a pointer.

from_fact: return err_ptr-encoded error on failure (non-null pointer with encoded error)
return null on failure, errno set to exxx

subject: libbpf_get_error() behaviour for NULL pointers
truth: libbpf_get_error() now returns -EINVAL when passed NULL, making NULL and ERR_PTR errors transparently handled.
quote: libbpf_get_error() will start to return -EINVAL for NULL cases, handling both ERR_PTR() and NULL cases transparently.

subject: xsk.c and xsk.h
truth: xsk.c and xsk.h have been removed from libbpf and moved into the libxdp library.
quote: xsk.{c,h} is moving into libxdp

subject: AF_XDP (xsk.h/xsk.c)
truth: Stop using xsk.h and xsk.c from libbpf; they are removed and moved to libxdp.
quote: AF_XDP parts of libbpf (xsk.h and xsk.c) will be removed from libbpf v1.0 and will become a part of libxdp.

subject: BPF program section name (SEC()) separator
truth: Stop using non-'/' separators in SEC() names; use '/' consistently (e.g. 'perf_event/whatever' not 'perf_event_whatever').
quote: Use '/' as a separator consistently. So no more "perf_event_whatever", only "perf_event/whatever".

subject: BPF program section name (SEC()) extra parameters
old: Allowing extra text beyond program type, e.g. 'xdp_my_prog' or 'cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage'
new: Only allow program type (and optional subtype after '/'), no extra parameters, e.g. 'xdp' not 'xdp_my_prog', 'cgroup_skb/ingress' not 'cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage'
truth: Stop appending extra parameters to SEC() names; use only the program type (e.g. 'xdp') or type/subtype (e.g. 'cgroup_skb/ingress') with no extra text.
quote: For section names not having any extra "parameters", don't allow anything extra beyond BPF program type. So no more "xdp_my_prog", only "xdp". And no "cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage", only "cgroup_skb/ingress".

from_fact: allowing extra text beyond program type, e.g. 'xdp_my_prog' or 'cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage'
only allow program type (and optional subtype after '/'), no extra parameters, e.g. 'xdp' not 'xdp_my_prog', 'cgroup_skb/ingress' not 'cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage'

subject: Legacy BPF map declaration (struct bpf_map_def in SEC("maps"))
truth: Stop using struct bpf_map_def in SEC("maps") for BPF map declarations; use only BTF-defined maps with SEC(".maps").
quote: Legacy fixed-layout (through struct bpf_map_def) BPF map declaration in BPF code, residing in SEC("maps") will be dropped. Only BTF-defined maps will be supported starting from v1.0.

subject: BPF_ANNOTATE_KV_PAIR macro
truth: Stop using BPF_ANNOTATE_KV_PAIR macro; it is dropped in favor of BTF-defined maps with __type(key, ...) and __type(value, ...).
quote: In the same vein, BPF_ANNOTATE_KV_PAIR(btf_map, int, struct ipv_counts) support will be dropped.

subject: BPF program pinning path
new: uses BPF program's C function name for filename part
truth: BPF program pinning path now uses the C function name instead of the section name for the filename part.
quote: We'll change this behavior to use BPF program's C function name for the filename part of the pinning path.

from_fact: uses bpf program's c function name for filename part

subject: .rodata sections
truth: All .rodata* sections are merged into a single .rodata map, eliminating separate maps for each read-only section.
quote: .rodata and other read-only sections (e.g., .rodata.str1.1) will be merged by libbpf into a final .rodata map

subject: .bss section
new: merged into .data map, accessed via skel->data
truth: .bss is merged into .data, so all non-read-only variables are accessed through skel->data->my_var instead of skel->bss->my_var.
quote: .bss will be merged into .data by libbpf at open time (and in BPF skeleton), so all the non-read-only variables will be addressed through consistent skel->data->my_var.

from_fact: merged into .data map, accessed via skel->data

subject: skel->bss
truth: skel->bss will be completely removed; bpftool will stop generating it after libbpf 1.0.
quote: Sometime around or after libbpf 1.0 release bpftool will be updated to stop generating skel->bss completely.

subject: special internal map names (.rodata, .data, .kconfig)
truth: Special internal map names no longer include the truncated object name prefix; they are just '.rodata', '.data', '.kconfig'.
quote: The proposal is to do away with this truncated prefix and settle on obvious '.rodata', '.data', '.kconfig' names.

subject: btf__finalize_data()
truth: btf__finalize_data() is deprecated and will be removed; it was only for internal libbpf use.
quote: btf__finalize_data() will be deprecated. It is used only for internal libbpf needs.

subject: btf_ext__reloc_func_info()
truth: Stop using btf_ext__reloc_func_info() as it is deprecated and incompatible with multiple BPF programs per section.
quote: btf_ext__reloc_func_info() is already marked deprecated and is not compatible with libbpf's support for multiple BPF programs per section

subject: btf_ext__reloc_line_info()
truth: Stop using btf_ext__reloc_line_info() as it is deprecated and being removed.
quote: btf_ext__reloc_line_info() is in the similar boat

subject: btf_ext__func_info_rec_size()
truth: Stop using btf_ext__func_info_rec_size() as it is being removed; implement record size extraction yourself if needed.
quote: btf_ext__func_info_rec_size() is just a specialized 'extractor' of record size for specific types of .BTF.ext sections

associative: btf ext function info record size

subject: btf__load()
truth: Replace btf__load() with btf__load_into_kernel() to upload BTF into the kernel.
quote: btf__load() and btf__get_from_id() are used to 'upload' and 'download' BTF into/from the kernel. I propose to deprecate these two naming variants and introduce two new ones, reflecting their relationship to the kernel. E.g., btf__load_into_kernel()

subject: struct btf_dedup_opts
old: struct btf_dedup_opts (non-OPTS struct with dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size fields)
new: OPTS-based struct with dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size dropped
truth: struct btf_dedup_opts is converted to an extensible OPTS struct; dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size fields are removed.
quote: [#281](https://github.com/libbpf/libbpf/issues/281) `struct btf_dedup_opts` should be converted into extensible OPTS struct, similar to those used throughout most of the libbpf APIs.

from_fact: struct btf_dedup_opts (non-opts struct with dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size fields)
opts-based struct with dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size dropped

subject: btf__dedup()
new: btf__dedup() accepting new OPTS-based struct with btf_ext supplied through opts
truth: btf__dedup() now accepts a new OPTS-based struct; the btf_ext argument is supplied through opts instead of separately.
quote: We can also use symbol versioning to introduce a new variant of btf__dedup(), accepting new-style opts struct. btf_ext argument would be supplied through opts (it rarely is provided).

from_fact: btf__dedup() accepting new opts-based struct with btf_ext supplied through opts

subject: struct btf_dump_opts and btf_dump__new()
old: struct btf_dump_opts (non-OPTS) and btf_dump__new() accepting old struct
new: OPTS-based struct btf_dump_opts and new version of btf_dump__new() accepting OPTS
truth: struct btf_dump_opts is converted to OPTS framework and btf_dump__new() now accepts the new OPTS-based struct.
quote: struct btf_dump_opts and btf_dump__new() will be converted to OPTS framework and new version of btf_dump__new() API will be introduced

from_fact: struct btf_dump_opts (non-opts) and btf_dump__new() accepting old struct
opts-based struct btf_dump_opts and new version of btf_dump__new() accepting opts

subject: bpf_create_map_xattr
truth: Stop using bpf_create_map_xattr(), bpf_create_map_node(), bpf_create_map_name(), bpf_create_map(), bpf_create_map_in_map_node(), and bpf_create_map_in_map(); use bpf_map_create() instead.
quote: Deprecate the whole zoo of bpf_create_map_xattr(), bpf_create_map_node(), bpf_create_map_name(), bpf_create_map(), bpf_create_map_in_map_node(), bpf_create_map_in_map() APIs in favor of a single unified OPTS-based one, with the name matching bpf syscall's command (BPF_MAP_CREATE)

subject: bpf_load_program_xattr
truth: Stop using bpf_load_program_xattr(), bpf_load_program(), and bpf_verify_program(); use bpf_prog_load() instead.
quote: Similarly, deprecate bpf_load_program_xattr(), bpf_load_program(), bpf_verify_program() in favor of a single unified new API

subject: bpf_prog_test_run_xattr
truth: Stop using bpf_prog_test_run_xattr() and bpf_prog_test_run(); use bpf_prog_test_run_opts() instead.
quote: bpf_prog_test_run_xattr() and bpf_prog_test_run() will be deprecated in favor of already existing bpf_prog_test_run_opts().

subject: bpf_load_btf
truth: Stop using bpf_load_btf(); use bpf_btf_load() with OPTS instead.
quote: bpf_load_btf() has inconsistent naming (similar to bpf_load_program() and bpf_create_map()), and it doesn't allow to specify log level. So deprecate bpf_load_btf() in favor of bpf_btf_load() with OPTS.

subject: bpf_object__open
truth: Stop using bpf_object__open(), bpf_object__open_buffer(), and bpf_object__open_xattr(); use bpf_object__open_file() and bpf_object__open_mem() instead.
quote: bpf_object__open(), bpf_object__open_buffer(), bpf_object__open_xattr() – deprecated in favor of bpf_object__open_file() and bpf_object__open_mem().

subject: struct bpf_object_open_opts
truth: Stop using relaxed_core_relocs in struct bpf_object_open_opts; it is no longer honored.
quote: Remove few options from struct bpf_object_open_opts: relaxed_core_relocs weren’t honored for a long time now, remove it;

subject: bpf_object__load_xattr()
truth: Stop using bpf_object__load_xattr(); use bpf_object__load() instead, providing extra options via open opts or setter APIs before loading.
quote: Deprecate bpf_object__load_xattr(). bpf_object__load() is enough. Anything else could be provided in open opts or set through setter APIs before the load.

subject: bpf_object__unload()
truth: Stop using bpf_object__unload(); use bpf_object__close() to unload and free resources in one operation, as objects are not re-loadable after unload.
quote: Deprecate bpf_object__unload(). BPF objects are not re-loadable after unload. Use bpf_object__close() to unload and free up resources in one operation.

subject: bpf_object__find_program_by_title()
truth: Stop using bpf_object__find_program_by_title(); iterate with bpf_object__for_each_program() and compare using bpf_program__section_name() instead.
quote: Deprecate bpf_object__find_program_by_title() as an API. Searching by title (i.e., section name) is ambiguous with multiple BPF programs per section. Use bpf_object__for_each_program() macro to loop over all BPF programs and compare using bpf_program__section_name().

subject: bpf_object__next()
truth: Stop using bpf_object__next() and bpf_object__for_each_safe(); applications should track their own bpf_objects for iteration.
quote: Deprecate bpf_object__next() API and bpf_object__for_each_safe() macro. There is little utility to it, but it’s not thread-safe. Any application that needs to iterate over all its bpf_objects can keep track of them on their own easily.

subject: bpf_object__set_priv() / bpf_object__priv()
truth: Stop using bpf_object__set_priv() and bpf_object__priv(); libbpf entities are not containers for user private data.
quote: Deprecate bpf_object__set_priv()/bpf_object__priv(), bpf_program__set_priv()/bpf_program__priv() and bpf_map__set_priv()/bpf_map__priv(). libbpf entities are not arbitrary containers for user’s private data.

associative: store private data on BPF object

subject: bpf_program__set_priv() / bpf_program__priv()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__set_priv() and bpf_program__priv(); libbpf entities are not containers for user private data.
quote: Deprecate bpf_object__set_priv()/bpf_object__priv(), bpf_program__set_priv()/bpf_program__priv() and bpf_map__set_priv()/bpf_map__priv(). libbpf entities are not arbitrary containers for user’s private data.

associative: store private data on BPF program

subject: bpf_map__set_priv() / bpf_map__priv()
truth: Stop using bpf_map__set_priv() and bpf_map__priv(); libbpf entities are not containers for user private data.
quote: Deprecate bpf_object__set_priv()/bpf_object__priv(), bpf_program__set_priv()/bpf_program__priv() and bpf_map__set_priv()/bpf_map__priv(). libbpf entities are not arbitrary containers for user’s private data.

associative: store private data on BPF map

subject: bpf_program__next()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__next(); use bpf_object__next_program() instead.
quote: bpf_program__next() and bpf_program__prev() are confusingly named. They are really 'methods' of bpf_object, so should be called bpf_object__next_program() and bpf_object__prev_program().

subject: bpf_program__prev()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__prev(); use bpf_object__prev_program() instead.
quote: bpf_program__next() and bpf_program__prev() are confusingly named. They are really 'methods' of bpf_object, so should be called bpf_object__next_program() and bpf_object__prev_program().

subject: bpf_map__for_each() macro
truth: Stop using bpf_map__for_each(); use bpf_object__for_each_map() instead.
quote: Remove `bpf_map__for_each()` macro in favor of better named `bpf_object__for_each_map()`.

subject: bpf_program__size()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__size(); use bpf_program__insn_cnt() to get the instruction count.
quote: `bpf_program__size()` is mildly confusing, though not broken by any means. Add `bpf_program__insn_cnt()` and `bpf_program__insns()` getters to get access to finalized BPF assembly of each BPF program.

subject: bpf_program__set_prep()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__set_prep(); use bpf_program__insns() and bpf_program__insn_cnt() to get raw instructions, then load with bpf_prog_load().
quote: `bpf_program__set_prep()` is an obscure, less-known API which adds unnecessary complexity to the public API and internal implementation. Deprecate it. For cases when someone do still want to adjust and/or clone BPF programs, it could be achieved by using new `bpf_program__insns()` and `bpf_program__insn_cnt()` APIs to get raw (but libbpf-processed for CO-RE, bpf-to-bpf calls, map relocations, etc) BPF instructions and proceed with low-level `bpf_prog_load()` API.

subject: bpf_map__resize()
truth: Stop using bpf_map__resize(); use bpf_map__set_max_entries() instead.
quote: Discourage `bpf_map__resize()`, which is an alias to more clearly named `bpf_map__set_max_entries()`.

subject: bpf_map__def()
truth: Stop using bpf_map__def(); use individual getter APIs for map properties instead.
quote: Discourage `bpf_map__def()`. It is rarely used and non-extensible. Provide individual getter APIs to compensate (if we are still missing some of them).

subject: bpf_map__get_pin_path()
truth: Stop using bpf_map__get_pin_path(); use bpf_map__pin_path() for consistent naming.
quote: Discourage `bpf_map__get_pin_path()` and use consistent naming for getter, `bpf_map__pin_path()`.

subject: bpf_prog_load() and bpf_prog_load_xattr()
truth: Stop using bpf_prog_load() and bpf_prog_load_xattr(); use bpf_object__open_mem() or bpf_object__open_file() followed by bpf_object__load() instead.
quote: Deprecate `bpf_prog_load()` and `bpf_prog_load_xattr()` in favor of `bpf_object__open_{mem, file}()` and `bpf_object__load()` combo.

subject: bpf_set_link_xdp_fd() / bpf_set_link_xdp_fd_opts() / bpf_get_link_xdp_id() / bpf_get_link_xdp_info()
truth: Stop using bpf_set_link_xdp_fd(), bpf_set_link_xdp_fd_opts(), bpf_get_link_xdp_id(), bpf_get_link_xdp_info(); use libbpf_xdp_set_prog_fd() and libbpf_xdp_get_info() instead.
quote: `bpf_set_link_xdp_fd()` / `bpf_set_link_xdp_fd_opts()` / `bpf_get_link_xdp_id()` / `bpf_get_link_xdp_info()` don’t follow libbpf naming guidelines. They are akin to object-less libbpf helpers, so should be called with `libbpf_` prefix. They also have a distinct low-level feel. So: Discourage them (move them to libbpf_legacy.h); Introduce OPTS-based `libbpf_xdp_set_prog_fd()` and `libbpf_xdp_get_info()` or something along those lines.

associative: get xdp program id from link

subject: bpf_printk() macro
new: bpf_printk() now attempts to use static global functions
truth: bpf_printk() now attempts to use static global functions, which can break on very old kernels; define BPF_NO_GLOBAL_DATA before including bpf_helpers.h if needed.
quote: bpf_printk() will now attempt to use static global functions, so on very old kernels this might break existing programs. Please #define BPF_NO_GLOBAL_DATA before #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> if that's the case for you.

from_fact: bpf_printk() now attempts to use static global functions

subject: BPF map definitions
truth: Stop using struct bpf_map_def for map definitions; use BTF-defined map definitions instead when LIBBPF_STRICT_MAP_DEFINITIONS is set.
quote: legacy BPF map definitions (using struct bpf_map_def) are deprecated when LIBBPF_STRICT_MAP_DEFINITIONS is passed to libbpf_set_strict_mode(). Please use BTF-defined map definitions.

subject: deprecated APIs and features
truth: Stop using all previously deprecated libbpf APIs and features; they have been removed entirely.
quote: All deprecated APIs and features removed!

subject: BPF_MAP_TYPE_USER_RINGBUF
truth: Stop relying on user-space ring buffer not being supported; it is now supported.
quote: user-space ring buffer (BPF_MAP_TYPE_USER_RINGBUF) support

subject: BPF_F_MMAPABLE flag for data maps
old: libbpf added BPF_F_MMAPABLE flag for all data maps
new: libbpf only adds BPF_F_MMAPABLE flag for data maps with global (non-static) vars
truth: Stop expecting BPF_F_MMAPABLE to be added for data maps with only static variables; it is now only added for maps with global (non-static) vars.
quote: libbpf will only add BPF_F_MMAPABLE flag for data maps with *global* (i.e., non-static) vars

from_fact: libbpf added bpf_f_mmapable flag for all data maps
libbpf only adds bpf_f_mmapable flag for data maps with global (non-static) vars

subject: BPF verifier log level and flags
old: libbpf modified/transformed BPF verifier log level and flags before passing to kernel
new: libbpf passes BPF verifier log level and flags to kernel as-is
truth: Stop relying on libbpf transforming verifier log level/flags; they are now passed through to the kernel unchanged.
quote: pass-through BPF verifier log level and flags to kernel as is

from_fact: libbpf modified/transformed bpf verifier log level and flags before passing to kernel
libbpf passes bpf verifier log level and flags to kernel as-is

subject: SEC definitions for tc programs
truth: Stop using SEC("tc"), SEC("action"), and SEC("classifier") as they are now legacy; use SEC("tc/egress"), SEC("tc/ingress"), SEC("tcx/egress"), or SEC("tcx/ingress") instead.
quote:
the following SEC definitions are now considered legacy:
    - `SEC("tc")`
    - `SEC("action")`
    - `SEC("classifier")`

subject: struct_ops SEC auto-creation default
old: SEC("struct_ops") and SEC("struct_ops.link") auto-create struct_ops maps by default
new: SEC("?.struct_ops") and SEC("?.struct_ops.link") default to NOT auto-create struct_ops map
truth: SEC("struct_ops") and SEC("struct_ops.link") now default to NOT auto-creating the struct_ops map; use SEC("struct_ops") without '?' to get the old auto-create behaviour.
quote: SEC("?.struct_ops") and SEC("?.struct_ops.link") are now supported, default to not auto-create struct_ops map

from_fact: sec("struct_ops") and sec("struct_ops.link") auto-create struct_ops maps by default
sec("?.struct_ops") and sec("?.struct_ops.link") default to not auto-create struct_ops map

subject: triple-underscore flavors for kfunc relocation
old: kfunc relocation resolved using exact function name including trailing underscores
new: triple-underscore suffix (___.*) is ignored when resolving kfunc relocations
truth: kfunc relocation resolution now ignores a triple-underscore suffix (___.*) on the function name, so a kfunc named 'foo___bar' will match a relocation for 'foo'.
quote: triple-underscore flavors for kfunc relocation: like with CO-RE structs `___.*` suffix is ignored when kfunc relocations are resolved

from_fact: kfunc relocation resolved using exact function name including trailing underscores
triple-underscore suffix (___.*) is ignored when resolving kfunc relocations

subject: faccessat() internal usage
truth: The internal use of faccessat() has been fixed, which breaks Android versions of libbpf.
quote: fix `faccessat()` internal usage, breaking Android versions of libbpf

subject: btf__get_map_kv_tids()
truth: Stop using btf__get_map_kv_tids() as it is being deprecated and removed.
quote: btf__get_map_kv_tids() doesn't make any sense now that BPF_ANNOTATE_KV_PAIR() is not supported

associative: get map key value type ids

subject: btf_ext__line_info_rec_size()
truth: Stop using btf_ext__line_info_rec_size() as it is being removed; implement record size extraction yourself if needed.
quote: btf_ext__line_info_rec_size(), same as above

associative: btf ext line info record size

subject: libbpf_find_kernel_btf()
truth: Replace libbpf_find_kernel_btf() with libbpf_load_vmlinux_btf() for loading vmlinux BTF.
quote: libbpf_find_kernel_btf() – its name should more correctly be libbpf_load_vmlinux_btf(), so the proposal is to add such a naming variant and discourage/deprecate the current one.

subject: bpf_prog_attach_xattr
truth: Stop using bpf_prog_attach_xattr(); use bpf_prog_attach_opts() instead.
quote: bpf_prog_attach_xattr()'s naming makes less and less sense with dropping all the xattr APIs. So we'll converge to the convention used in high-level APIs and use bpf_prog_attach_opts() API name.

subject: struct bpf_object_open_opts
truth: Stop using attach_prog_fd in struct bpf_object_open_opts; use bpf_program__set_attach_target() on individual programs after opening the object instead.
quote: remove attach_prog_fd, it only works for a case of bpf_object containing a single BPF program or if all BPF programs are attached to the same target. Neither is a typical situation, so this option just leads to more confusion. Use bpf_program__set_attach_target() on individual bpf_program after bpf_object is open.

subject: bpf_map__next()
truth: Stop using bpf_map__next(); use bpf_object__next_map() instead.
quote: bpf_map__next() and bpf_map__prev() suffer from the same problem. Deal with them similarly.

subject: bpf_map__prev()
truth: Stop using bpf_map__prev(); use bpf_object__prev_map() instead.
quote: bpf_map__next() and bpf_map__prev() suffer from the same problem. Deal with them similarly.

subject: bpf_program__title()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__title(); use bpf_program__section_name() instead.
quote: Deprecate bpf_program__title() in favor of bpf_program__section_name(). 'Title' term is confusing and unconventional, it's SEC() in code and 'section name' everywhere else.

subject: bpf_object__find_map_by_offset()
truth: Stop using bpf_object__find_map_by_offset(); it has been removed entirely.
quote: Remove `bpf_object__find_map_by_offset()`. API created with simplistic assumptions about BPF map definitions. It hasn’t worked for a while, so just remove it finally.

subject: bpf_program__pin_instance()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__pin_instance(); it is deprecated and will be removed.
quote: Deprecate `bpf_program__pin_instance()`, `bpf_program__unpin_instance()` and `bpf_program__nth_fd()`. With no `bpf_program__set_prep()`, there is no concept of multiple bpf_program instances.

subject: bpf_program__unpin_instance()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__unpin_instance(); it is deprecated and will be removed.
quote: Deprecate `bpf_program__pin_instance()`, `bpf_program__unpin_instance()` and `bpf_program__nth_fd()`. With no `bpf_program__set_prep()`, there is no concept of multiple bpf_program instances.

subject: bpf_program__nth_fd()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__nth_fd(); it is deprecated and will be removed.
quote: Deprecate `bpf_program__pin_instance()`, `bpf_program__unpin_instance()` and `bpf_program__nth_fd()`. With no `bpf_program__set_prep()`, there is no concept of multiple bpf_program instances.

subject: bpf_program__load()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__load(); it is deprecated because individual BPF programs cannot be loaded in isolation.
quote: Deprecate `bpf_program__load()`. In general, it’s impossible for libbpf to load an individual BPF program in isolation. libbpf fully embraced the concept of bpf_object as a collection of related bpf_programs and bpf_maps (and global variables, kconfig, ksym, etc). So this API is just confusing and impossible to support properly.

subject: bpf_map__is_offload_neutral()
truth: Stop using bpf_map__is_offload_neutral(); it is deprecated and likely broken.
quote: Deprecate `bpf_map__is_offload_neutral()`. It’s most probably broken already. PERF_EVENT_ARRAY isn’t the only map that’s not suitable for hardware offloading. Unlikely anyone is using this and it is a maintenance burden, if we were to make it correct.

subject: bpf_perf_event_read_simple()
truth: Stop using bpf_perf_event_read_simple(); use perf_buffer__poll() and perf_buffer__consume() instead.
quote: bpf_perf_event_read_simple() is a low-level and dangerous API that could be used to implement custom perf buffer consumption, but is hard to actually use correctly. Deprecate it.

subject: bpf_program__get_prog_info_linear()
truth: Stop using bpf_program__get_prog_info_linear() and related APIs; they will be renamed to libbpf_-prefixed names because they don't operate on bpf_program objects.
quote: bpf_program__get_prog_info_linear() doesn't even declare that it is expecting enum bpf_prog_info_array and just goes with generic __u64. They seem to be only used in perf and bpftool, so updating all users seems doable. At the very least, I'd fix the naming to be libbpf_ prefixed.
